debug-plan.executive-summary:
Six systemic failures prevented end-to-end RunPod GPU smoke tests. Three are P0 (launch blockers), two are P1 (runtime failures), one is P2 (reliability). All six are structural — not one-off bugs. Each requires a targeted code/config change plus a regression test to prevent recurrence.
Execution order: Issues 1→5→2→3→4→6 (dependency-ordered, not priority-ordered). Estimated implementation: ~30 min code + tests, then live RunPod validation.

issue.title: SkyPilot jobs controller disk_size exceeds RunPod 40 GB max
issue.root-cause: sky jobs launch creates a controller VM on RunPod. SkyPilot's default controller disk_size is 50 GB but RunPod rejects anything >40 GB. The task YAML's disk_size: 40 only affects the WORKER, not the controller. Without ~/.sky/config.yaml capping the controller disk, sky jobs launch fails before the worker even starts.
issue.evidence: RunPod API rejected disk_size=50 during previous sky jobs launch attempt. The smoke_test_gpu.yaml fix (disk_size: 40) only fixed the worker node.

fix.action: Create SkyPilot user config capping controller resources for RunPod
fix.code: # ~/.sky/config.yaml — SkyPilot user-level config # Caps jobs controller disk to 40 GB (RunPod max) jobs: controller: resources: disk_size: 40 cloud: runpod

test.assertion: Verify ~/.sky/config.yaml exists and caps controller disk ≤40

reviewer-notes.R1: What if user has existing ~/.sky/config.yaml? → Merge, don't overwrite.
reviewer-notes.R2: SkyPilot reads this file on every launch. Controller resources key is documented.
reviewer-notes.R3: Idempotent — creating same file twice is safe.

issue.title: EXPERIMENT env var uses ${MODEL_FAMILY} — OmegaConf interprets as config interpolation
issue.root-cause:
smoke_test_gpu.yaml has `EXPERIMENT: smoke_${MODEL_FAMILY}` in envs section. SkyPilot envs resolves ${} from the API server environment, NOT from other envs keys. MODEL_FAMILY is defined in the SAME envs block, so ${MODEL_FAMILY} is NOT resolved by SkyPilot — the literal string "smoke_${MODEL_FAMILY}" is passed to the VM.
train_flow.py line 795 reads EXPERIMENT from os.environ, passes it to compose_experiment_config(experiment_name=...). Hydra-zen/OmegaConf sees ${MODEL_FAMILY} and throws ConfigCompositionException — it's not a valid OmegaConf interpolation key.
The argparse fallback (line 828) then runs with wrong config: no smoke experiment config, wrong epochs, wrong splits = wasted GPU time.
issue.evidence: Previous RunPod run: ConfigCompositionException: "Error resolving interpolation 'smoke_${MODEL_FAMILY}'". Training continued on argparse fallback with wrong config.

fix.action: 1. REMOVE EXPERIMENT from envs section entirely. 2. Compute EXPERIMENT in run: block using shell variable expansion (NOT ${}). 3. Export EXPERIMENT before calling train_flow.py.
fix.code:
# In envs section: DELETE the EXPERIMENT line entirely. # EXPERIMENT must NOT be in envs — SkyPilot can't resolve intra-envs references.
# In run section: compute EXPERIMENT from MODEL_FAMILY using shell run: | source $HOME/.local/bin/env cd /app
# Compute EXPERIMENT from MODEL_FAMILY (shell expansion, not SkyPilot ${}) export EXPERIMENT="smoke_${MODEL_FAMILY}"
# UUID isolation via HYDRA_OVERRIDES (separate from Hydra config name) export EXPERIMENT_UUID=$(uv run python -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:8])") export HYDRA_OVERRIDES="experiment_name=smoke_test_${EXPERIMENT_UUID}_${MODEL_FAMILY}"
echo "=== MinIVess GPU Smoke Test ===" echo "Model: ${MODEL_FAMILY}" echo "Hydra config: ${EXPERIMENT}" echo "MLflow experiment: smoke_test_${EXPERIMENT_UUID}_${MODEL_FAMILY}" echo "MLflow server: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}"
uv run python -m minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow

test.assertion: test_experiment_not_in_envs: EXPERIMENT must NOT appear in envs section. test_experiment_computed_in_run: run block must contain 'export EXPERIMENT='.

reviewer-notes.R1: What about GIT_BRANCH: ${GIT_BRANCH:-main}? → That's fine: GIT_BRANCH is resolved from caller env (--env-file .env), not from intra-envs. The :-default syntax is SkyPilot-supported for caller env vars.
reviewer-notes.R2: Shell ${MODEL_FAMILY} in run: block works because SkyPilot sets MODEL_FAMILY as a real shell env var before run: executes. This is the correct mechanism.
reviewer-notes.R3: Re-running with different MODEL_FAMILY produces correct EXPERIMENT name.

issue.title: Makefile smoke-test-gpu uses -e (invalid flag) — must use --env
issue.root-cause:
Makefile line 83-84 uses `-e MODEL_FAMILY=...` and `-e GIT_BRANCH=...`. SkyPilot CLI uses `--env KEY=VALUE`, not `-e`. This was a Docker-ism. Additionally, env vars like DVC_S3_* and MLFLOW_CLOUD_* from .env are NOT passed to SkyPilot at all — they need --env-file .env or individual --env flags.
SkyPilot's envs section with ${VAR} resolves from the API server daemon's environment — NOT the caller's shell. --env-file loads the caller's .env into the SkyPilot API server's resolution context.
issue.evidence: Previous session: SkyPilot passed literal "${DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL}" to RunPod VM because API server had no DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL in its environment.

fix.action: Fix smoke-test-gpu target: 1. Use --env (not -e) for MODEL_FAMILY and GIT_BRANCH 2. Add --env-file .env to pass DVC_S3_* and MLFLOW_CLOUD_* vars
fix.code: smoke-test-gpu: ## Launch GPU smoke test on RunPod (MODEL=sam3_vanilla, BRANCH=main) @echo "=== Launching GPU smoke test: $(MODEL) on RunPod RTX 4090 ===" sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env-file .env \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=$(or $(MODEL),sam3_vanilla) \ --env GIT_BRANCH=$(or $(BRANCH),$(shell git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)) \ -y

reviewer-notes.R1: Does sky jobs launch support --env-file? → Yes, documented in SkyPilot CLI.
reviewer-notes.R2: --env-file .env loads ALL vars from .env into SkyPilot's resolution context. ${DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL} in envs section will now resolve correctly.

issue.title: DVC preprocess stage not frozen — generic dvc pull fails
issue.root-cause:
dvc.yaml has 3 stages: download (frozen), preprocess (NOT frozen), validate_data. The preprocess stage depends on data/processed/minivess/ which has no cache on the UpCloud S3 remote. A bare `dvc pull` tries ALL stages and exits non-zero when the preprocess stage's outputs aren't cached.
The YAML already uses `dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r upcloud` (targeted pull), but the preprocess stage should also be frozen to prevent accidental full-pull failures.

fix.action: Freeze preprocess stage (it's not used in smoke tests)

test.assertion: test_dvc_preprocess_frozen: preprocess stage must be frozen

issue.title: Run block has no guards verifying setup completed successfully
issue.root-cause:
If sky exec is used (which skips setup:), or if setup partially fails but the run block still executes, critical prerequisites are missing: - /app directory doesn't exist - splits.json not copied - DVC data not pulled - uv not installed
The run block blindly proceeds and fails with cryptic errors.

fix.action: Add pre-flight guards at the top of run: block
fix.code:
run: | source $HOME/.local/bin/env cd /app
# Guard: verify setup completed (catches sky exec without setup) if [ ! -f configs/splits/splits.json ]; then echo "FATAL: splits.json missing — setup did not complete. Use 'sky launch' not 'sky exec'." exit 1 fi if [ ! -d data/raw/minivess/imagesTr ]; then echo "FATAL: Training data missing — DVC pull failed in setup." exit 1 fi

test.assertion: test_run_has_setup_guards: run block must check splits.json and training data

issue.title: Spot preemption kills training with no recovery
issue.root-cause:
sky launch does NOT auto-recover from spot preemption. Only sky jobs launch has built-in spot recovery (re-queues the job on a new instance).
The Makefile already uses sky jobs launch (correct), but the previous manual debugging used sky launch (incorrect for spot). This is a documentation/workflow issue — the launch comment in the YAML should be clearer.

fix.action: Update header comment to emphasize sky jobs launch (not sky launch). Add WARNING comment about sky launch vs sky jobs launch.

test.assertion: test_yaml_recommends_jobs_launch: Header must say "sky jobs launch"

execution-order.step: Remove EXPERIMENT from envs, compute in run block. This is the critical Hydra/OmegaConf fix that caused wrong config last time.
Add run-block guards (depends on step 1 since we're editing the same file).
Update YAML header comments for sky jobs launch.
Write regression tests for ALL 6 fixes.
Run make test-staging to verify all tests pass.
Run make smoke-test-preflight for live validation.

cost-analysis.item: Smoke test (2 epochs, 4 volumes): ~5 min per model
Setup overhead (clone, install, DVC pull): ~5 min
Budget for debugging retries: 3 attempts max = ~$0.36
